relevant_files.role: Main SecurityScanner implementation using regex-based line-by-line scanning
PatternEngine with 14 regex patterns for vulnerability detection
Embedded regex pattern definitions (hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, XSS, crypto)
Core types: Finding, PatternDefinition, Severity, Confidence
Integration tests including multi-line limitation test from PR #736
Performance benchmarks for security scanning (<10ms target)
Test fixture with single-line SQL injection patterns
Workspace configuration - would add tree-sitter deps here
Core crate dependencies - currently uses regex = "1"

conventions.code_style: Rust with SPDX license headers, comprehensive doc comments
conventions.testing: Unit tests in same file, integration tests in tests/, fixtures in tests/security_fixtures/
conventions.patterns: JSON-embedded regex patterns with metadata (severity, CWE, description)
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications
conventions.benchmarking: Criterion.rs for performance tests with <10ms target
conventions.api_design: Builder pattern with bon, async traits with async-trait
conventions.documentation: Conventional Commits, DCO sign-off, GPG signing required

patterns.notes: Fast but cannot detect multi-line vulnerabilities where source/sink span lines
14 patterns covering secrets, injection, weak crypto, path traversal
Security scanning results exportable to SARIF for CI integration
Explicitly documents architectural limitation, should be updated when fixed

anti_patterns.issue: Cannot detect tainted data flows across multiple lines
anti_patterns.example: SQL injection where user input is assigned on line 10, used in query on line 15

related_issues.summary: Evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection - main feature request
Add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation (CLOSED)
Merged PR implementing the negative test from #735

constraints: Must maintain <10ms scanning performance target (per criterion benchmark)
Binary size increase from grammar crates (trade-off)
Hybrid approach preferred per issue - keep regex for simple patterns
Language-specific grammars needed for each supported language
Query syntax learning curve (Scheme-like tree-sitter queries)
Data-flow/taint tracking requires custom logic on top of AST
test_coverage: Comprehensive: unit tests in each module, integration tests with fixtures, negative tests for limitations, criterion benchmarks for performance. Key test: test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected documents the exact limitation tree-sitter would solve.

library_findings.name: tree-sitter
library_findings.notes: Incremental parsing, fast re-parsing on edits, mature ecosystem

library_findings.name: tree-sitter-rust
library_findings.notes: Would enable AST-based Rust vulnerability detection

library_findings.name: tree-sitter-python
library_findings.role: Python grammar (likely needed for Python support)
library_findings.notes: Part of core tree-sitter org, well-maintained

library_findings.name: tree-sitter-grep
library_findings.notes: Could inspire query patterns or be used as reference implementation

library_findings.name: type-sitter
library_findings.role: Generate typed wrappers for tree-sitter grammars
library_findings.notes: Could reduce boilerplate when working with AST nodes

approaches.title: Approach 1: Hybrid Scanner with Pluggable Backends
approaches.summary: Refactor SecurityScanner to support both regex and tree-sitter backends. Keep regex for simple line-based patterns (hardcoded secrets), add tree-sitter for multi-line data-flow patterns (SQL injection). Add a new AstScanner trait with implementations per language.
pros: Can incrementally migrate patterns to AST as needed
Maintains fast path for simple patterns
cons: Need to decide per-pattern which backend to use
approaches.complexity: Medium - requires trait abstraction and backend selection logic

approaches.title: Approach 2: Tree-Sitter Query Patterns (Full Migration)
approaches.summary: Replace regex patterns entirely with tree-sitter queries. Convert all 14 patterns to .scm query files. Add language detection and grammar loading per-file. Implement a unified TreeSitterScanner that parses files and runs queries.
cons: Major rewrite of security module (~500-800 lines)
All 14 patterns need conversion to query syntax
Need grammars for all supported languages (size bloat)
approaches.complexity: High - complete rewrite of pattern engine

approaches.title: Approach 3: Spike with Feature Flag
approaches.summary: Implement a minimal tree-sitter proof-of-concept behind a Cargo feature flag. Add one multi-line SQL injection pattern using tree-sitter-rust only. Compare detection quality and performance against regex. Use findings to inform full implementation.
pros: Low risk - behind feature flag, doesn't affect production
approaches.complexity: Low-Medium - focused spike with limited scope
files_to_touch: tests/ - add spike-specific tests for multi-line detection

recommendation: Recommend Approach 3 (Spike with Feature Flag) first, then Approach 1 (Hybrid). The spike validates tree-sitter integration with minimal risk - add tree-sitter-rust only, detect one multi-line SQL injection pattern, measure performance. If successful, implement Approach 1's hybrid architecture which provides the best of both worlds: regex for fast/simple patterns, tree-sitter for data-flow detection. This aligns with the issue's 'Suggested Approach' and maintains the existing <10ms benchmark target by keeping regex for patterns that don't need AST awareness.
